programmes.show_description: OJ is live through the night from the Salford Riviera with Midnight Mastermind, Wish You Were There, Midnight Caller and a huge Ravio 2 to end the week.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: Your name is on the guest list so join Vernon Kay for an hour of pure club floorfillers!
It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Step Back in Time and Airdrum Anthems. It's your official early morning weekend warm up! What can't you wait for?!
It's Big Guest Friday with Joel Dommett, Johannes Radebe and Alison Moyet in the studio! Plus, the Wonder Years is a Country Special.
The Charlatans' frontman and solo artist Tim Burgess joins Vernon for his final two song selections of the week in Tracks Of My Years. Plus, two listeners take on Ten To The Top.
Jeremy is joined by Kezia Gill as part of Radio 2's celebration of the Country to Country festival on Radio 2.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
It's All Request Friday, and Sara wants to hear from you on 88291 or sara.cox@bbc.co.uk.
Get into the groove and dance yourself dizzy at Michelle's Fabulous Friday party.
Michelle's Mood is...DO YOU? Your 30 minutes of non-stop bops is about to POP!
Sophie's playing two hours of tunes to move to! There's more Request Line action and having just got back from touring Oz, she takes a trip down under in the Disco Wonderland.

programmes.show_name: The Rock Show with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: Shaun curates an hour of the best rock ever heard on Radio 2.

programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: Romesh hears from conscious rapper Brother Ali, who shares the songs that have shaped him in a 321, as well as a cut from his latest album 'Satisfied Soul'.
Tony Blackburn's smooth audio sunshine returns with more trailblazers of Soul - including sensational tracks from Sister Sledge, Jill Scott, The Supremes and Tavares.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1976.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1980.
UB40 performing live at Brighton's International Centre in 1985. Featuring Don't Break My Heart, One In Ten, Please Don't Make Me Cry, Red Red Wine and I Got You Babe.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin reflects Radio 2 Country with a Calm Country Unwinds Mix featuring soothing sounds from some country great.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from The Move, Roy Orbison and Aretha Franklin. This week, the top three comes from 1968.
Jessica Gunning (Baby Reindeer), Nonso Anozie (Game of Thrones) & Simon Farnaby (Paddington) chat about the new Enid Blyton adaptation - The Magic Faraway Tree.
Romesh brings the laughs, soundtracked by great music. As well as chatting to brilliant guests, he's sharing your hilarious stories, so you can start your Saturday with a smile!
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Bob Harris and Jackson Dean live from the O2 for the biggest country event of the year! They're backstage at C2C with chat and music from Keith Urban, Russell Dickerson and more!
Shaun Keaveny sits in with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: With Big Country's Bruce Watson on My 80s Moments and a Pointer Sisters Mastermix.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Join Fearne for a nostalgia drenched celebration of the best 90s music and pop culture.
